channel.title: International: Top News And Analysis
channel.description: CNBC International is the world leader for news on business, technology, China, trade, oil prices, the Middle East and markets.

item.title: Oil surges above $100 a barrel; Trump says 'small price to pay' for defeating Iran
item.description: Gulf Arab oil producers are cutting production as they run out of storage space because they can't export through the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Iran names Ayatollah Khamenei's son, Mojtaba, as new supreme leader: Media reports
item.description: Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates report more strikes on civilian targets as U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran continue.

item.title: China says 'thorough preparations' needed as Trump-Xi meeting hangs in the balance amid Iran war
item.description: China's top diplomat Wang Yi signaled preparations are underway for a planned meeting between the presidents of the U.S. and China.

item.title: Global week ahead: Diplomacy in ruins as G7 meets on Iran
item.description: G7 finance ministers and central bank governors are set to convene for a key meeting as diplomatic tensions escalate over the Iran conflict.

item.title: I've studied over 200 kids—parents who raise emotionally intelligent kids regularly ask their children 9 questions
item.description: Raising emotionally intelligent kids starts with everyday conversations at home, says child psychologist Reem Raouda, who has studied over 200 kids. Here are some questions parents can use to get started.

item.title: Register now: Applications open for the World's Top Fintech Companies 2026
item.description: CNBC and Statista chart the top fintech players from around the world, ranging from startups to Big Tech names.

item.title: Iran's internet blackout extends into second week: NetBlocks
item.description: Iran's government cut internet connectivity shortly after start of U.S.-Israeli attacks on Feb. 28.

item.title: One year after Trump’s sovereignty threats, Canadians keep ‘elbows up’
item.description: An unusual swell of Canadian patriotism seen after Trump's threats and tariffs last year has evolved into a new social and economic order.

item.title: States led by New York sue to block Trump's latest tariffs, calling them an illegal end run around Supreme Court
item.description: The move from the state AGs — part of the successful effort to block Trump's original tariffs — adds to the ongoing uncertainty created by his tariff policies.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: Energy, airlines and now over $50 billion in remittances to India at risk as Middle East conflict deepens
item.description: India can't seem to escape from the fallout of the escalating conflict in the Middle East. After energy and aviation, remittances could be the latest worry.

item.title: Oil surges 35% this week for biggest gain in futures trading history dating back to 1983
item.description: The oil market is worried that Gulf countries will have to shut production if tankers are unable to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump wants U.S. Navy to escort tankers through the Gulf. Why that plan may not work
item.description: A backup of oil tankers in the Persian Gulf is disrupting supplies, and will only get worse the longer the Strait of Hormuz is closed.

item.title: Zealand's stock plummets after disappointing drug result. Its CEO tells CNBC people need to focus less on the 'weight loss Olympics'
item.description: Zealand and Roche are jointly developing the drug petrelintide which in a mid-stage study led to a worse-than-expected 10.7% weight-loss over 42 weeks.

item.title: Amazon says Anthropic’s Claude still OK for AWS customers to use outside defense work
item.description: Amazon joined Microsoft and Google in continue to offer Anthropic's Claude AI technology to customers after the Pentagon deemed it a "supply chain risk."

item.title: Trump admin announces $20 billion reinsurance program for oil tankers during Iran war
item.description: Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill as the Iran war engulfs the region.

item.title: Palantir rallies 15% for the week as Iran war boosts prospects, muting Anthropic concern
item.description: Palantir's stock had its best week since August and outperformed all of its large-cap tech peers after the U.S. attacked Iran.

item.title: Google joins Microsoft in telling users Anthropic is still available outside defense projects
item.description: Cloud vendors are letting customers know that Anthropic's popular AI tools can still be accessed after the Department of Defense blacklisted the company.

item.title: Trump tariffs: Customs and Border Protection tells judge it can't comply with refund order
item.description: CBP told Judge Richard Eaton that the technology upgrades it plans would save more than 4 million man-hours in processing refunds for Trump's tariffs.

item.title: Samsung reveals first details of its AI smart glasses to CNBC
item.description: Samsung's AI smart glasses will be launched in 2026 and will mark the company's first foray into the product category.

item.title: Marvell stock surges 18% as CEO points to continuing AI demand: 'Do you see me blinking?'
item.description: Marvell shares surged on Friday after the company beat earnings expectations and issued strong guidance on continuing AI demand.

item.title: How Iran and Venezuela strikes transform the Trump-Xi trade talks
item.description: The Iran war and the U.S. attack in Venezuela show a Trump administration more willing to eschew diplomacy and take military action.

item.title: Toyota, Hyundai and Chinese automakers expected to be most impacted by Iran war
item.description: While regional sales will be impacted, the closing of the Strait of Hormuz and rising oil prices will have ripple effects across the global automotive industry.

item.title: U.S. payrolls unexpectedly fell by 92,000 in February; unemployment rate rises to 4.4%
item.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to increase 50,000 in February while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%.

item.title: Maersk, a bellwether for global trade, suspends two key shipping services due to Iran war
item.description: The update comes as the Iran war enters its seventh day and the escalating regional conflict continues to disrupt global supply chains.

item.title: The Tech Download: Data centers become military targets as Iran war rages on
item.description: Infrastructure underpinning digital services has been pulled into the conflict in the Middle East

item.title: U.S. offers India a 30-day waiver for buying Russian oil as Iran war deepens energy supply worries
item.description: U.S. has given India a waiver to buy Russian oil for 30 days as conflict in Middle East impacts global energy supply.

item.title: UAE mulls freezing Iranian assets as Middle East conflict escalates: WSJ
item.description: The UAE is considering freezing billions of dollars of Iranian assets held in the Gulf state, potentially severing one of Tehran's most critical economic lifelines.

item.title: German defense firm Renk CEO says Iran war could drive 'increasing demand' in the Middle East
item.description: The current conflict in the Middle East could benefit Renk's business, CEO Alexander Sagel said on an earnings call.

item.title: Iran's Shahed drone: How 'the poor man’s cruise missile’ is shaping Tehran’s retaliation
item.description: After years on the front lines of Russia's invasion of Ukraine, the Iranian Shahed-136 drone is at the center of Tehran's retaliation against recent U.S. strikes.

item.title: Dubai real estate magnate: There could be a property cooldown, but I don't see it
item.description: Mohamed Alabbar acknowledged the attacks but insisted investors were still confident in Dubai and that "smart capital" will continue to flow into the city.
